description: Conditional expectation properties from Saporito Ch 2.1 (measure_theory).

theorems.name: Linearity of Conditional Expectation
theorems.description: Proposition 2.1.5(2): E[αX + Y | G] = αE[X|G] + E[Y|G] a.s.

code.lean:
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Function.ConditionalExpectation.Basic

open MeasureTheory

-- Linearity of conditional expectation: μ[c•f + g | m] =ᵐ[μ] c•μ[f|m] + μ[g|m]
-- Uses Mathlib's `condExp_add` and `condExp_smul`.
theorem condexp_linear {α : Type*} {m m₀ : MeasurableSpace α} {μ : Measure α}
    (f g : α → ℝ) (c : ℝ)
    (hf : Integrable f μ) (hg : Integrable g μ) :
    μ[c • f + g | m] =ᵐ[μ] c • μ[f | m] + μ[g | m] := by
  have h1 : μ[c • f + g | m] =ᵐ[μ] μ[c • f | m] + μ[g | m] :=
    condExp_add (hf.smul c) hg m
  have h2 : μ[c • f | m] =ᵐ[μ] c • μ[f | m] := condExp_smul c f m
  filter_upwards [h1, h2] with x hx1 hx2
  rw [hx1, Pi.add_apply, hx2]
  rfl

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: Tower Property
theorems.description: Proposition 2.1.11(6): For H ⊆ G ⊆ F, E[E[X|G] | H] = E[E[X|H] | G] = E[X|H]

code.lean:
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Function.ConditionalExpectation.Basic

open MeasureTheory

-- Tower property: μ[μ[f|m₂]|m₁] =ᵐ[μ] μ[f|m₁] for m₁ ≤ m₂.
-- (Mathlib statement is almost-everywhere equality.)
theorem condexp_tower {α : Type*} {m₁ m₂ m₀ : MeasurableSpace α} {μ : Measure α}
    (hm₁₂ : m₁ ≤ m₂) (hm₂ : m₂ ≤ m₀) [SigmaFinite (μ.trim hm₂)]
    {f : α → ℝ} :
    μ[μ[f | m₂] | m₁] =ᵐ[μ] μ[f | m₁] :=
  condExp_condExp_of_le hm₁₂ hm₂

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: Pulling Out What's Known
theorems.description: Proposition 2.1.11(7): If Y is G-measurable and XY ∈ L¹, then E[XY | G] = Y · E[X | G]

code.lean:
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Function.ConditionalExpectation.Real

open MeasureTheory

-- Pulling out a known factor: μ[f * g | m] =ᵐ[μ] f * μ[g | m]
-- when f is m-strongly-measurable. Uses Mathlib's `condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left`.
theorem condexp_pull_out {α : Type*} {m m₀ : MeasurableSpace α} {μ : Measure α}
    {f g : α → ℝ}
    (hf : StronglyMeasurable[m] f) (hfg : Integrable (f * g) μ) (hg : Integrable g μ) :
    μ[f * g | m] =ᵐ[μ] f * μ[g | m] :=
  condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left hf hfg hg

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: Independence Implies E[X|G] = E[X]
theorems.description: Proposition 2.1.11(8): If X is independent of G, then E[X | G] = E[X] a.s.

code.lean:
import Mathlib.Probability.ConditionalExpectation

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

-- Independence ⇒ μ[f | m₂] =ᵐ[μ] (fun _ => ∫ x, f x ∂μ).
-- Mathlib's `condExp_indep_eq` requires: f is m₁-measurable and m₁ ⊥ m₂.
theorem condexp_indep {Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ℝ E]
    [CompleteSpace E]
    {m₁ m₂ m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {μ : Measure Ω} {f : Ω → E}
    (hle₁ : m₁ ≤ m) (hle₂ : m₂ ≤ m) [SigmaFinite (μ.trim hle₂)]
    (hf : StronglyMeasurable[m₁] f) (hindp : Indep m₁ m₂ μ) :
    μ[f | m₂] =ᵐ[μ] fun _ => μ[f] :=
  condExp_indep_eq hle₁ hle₂ hf hindp

metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around an existing library theorem closely matching the benchmark statement.

theorems.name: Conditional Jensen's Inequality
theorems.description: Proposition 2.1.11(9): For convex φ: ℝ → ℝ, E[φ(X) | G] ≥ φ(E[X | G]) a.s.

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory

/-- Proposition 2.1.11(9), conditional Jensen's inequality: for convex
    `φ : ℝ → ℝ` with `X` and `φ ∘ X` integrable,
    `φ(E[X | m]) ≤ᵐ E[φ(X) | m]`. Direct invocation of Mathlib's
    `ConvexOn.map_condExp_le_of_finiteDimensional` at `E = ℝ` — bare convexity
    suffices (convex functions on a finite-dimensional space are continuous,
    hence lower-semicontinuous); Mathlib does the real work. -/
theorem conditional_jensen_inequality
    {α : Type*} {m₀ : MeasurableSpace α} {μ : Measure α} [IsFiniteMeasure μ]
    {m : MeasurableSpace α} (hm : m ≤ m₀) {φ : ℝ → ℝ} {X : α → ℝ}
    (hφ_cvx : ConvexOn ℝ Set.univ φ)
    (hX_int : Integrable X μ)
    (hφX_int : Integrable (φ ∘ X) μ) :
    φ ∘ μ[X | m] ≤ᵐ[μ] μ[φ ∘ X | m] := by
  haveI : SigmaFinite (μ.trim hm) := inferInstance
  exact hφ_cvx.map_condExp_le_of_finiteDimensional hm hX_int hφX_int

metadata.lean_note: Wraps Mathlib's ConvexOn.map_condExp_le_of_finiteDimensional at E = ℝ. The former in-repo explicit-subgradient derivation (Foundations/CondExpJensen.lean) was removed in the 2026-06-03 audit: Mathlib's CondJensen proves the textbook statement from bare convexity (no subgradient API needed), and the subgradient version (bounded g ⇒ Lipschitz φ only) was strictly weaker.
metadata.formalization_scope: Direct Lean wrapper around Mathlib's conditional Jensen (ConvexOn.map_condExp_le_of_finiteDimensional), statement-identical to the textbook proposition. Relabeled full->library_wrapper in the 2026-06-03 audit; the library does the real work.
